rationale: The query/hyp engine is a probability-generating-function formalism: encode a degree/functionality distribution as a PGF and self-consistently compose it to locate a percolation-type threshold (giant component / gel point), then read off component-size or molecular-weight distributions from derivatives of that generating function. The candidate instead computes an equilibrium Ising-model critical temperature, magnetization, susceptibility, and specific heat on the same class of random graphs — spin thermodynamics, not generating-function branching combinatorics for connectivity/component structure — so "arbitrary degree distribution" and "phase transition" are shared vocabulary, not shared machinery (method_coherence FALSE). The candidate's actual object is still a random network with a given degree distribution studied for a network-science property (magnetic ordering), which resembles the query's own object/field (random graph / complex-network theory) far more than it resembles hyp_generic_object (branched macromolecules with a functionality distribution in polymer gelation) — so no genuine cross-field transfer occurred (object_difference FALSE).
